slides.title: Growth Engine
slides.body:
Week 3 · Day 4

Build Capacity.
Multiply Impact.

Financial Entrepreneurship
slides.speakerNotes:
Act 1 open (45 min). Hero slide — venture accelerator tone, not recruitment.
Recap: Client Experience → Business Engine → today Growth Engine → tomorrow Financial Engine → Friday Venture Pitch.

slides.title: What Did You Learn Yesterday?
slides.body:
Reflect on your Business Engine.

• Biggest insight
• Biggest surprise
• One thing you will improve
slides.speakerNotes:
Pair-share 3 minutes. Interns capture in Playbook Growth Engine worksheet opening reflection.
Bridge: yesterday you designed how the engine runs — today you design how it scales.
discussionQuestions: What surprised you about your Business Engine numbers?
What would break first if demand doubled?

slides.title: Why Businesses Stop Growing
slides.body:
Businesses don't fail because demand disappears.

They fail because capacity stops growing.
slides.speakerNotes:
One-page reading in Playbook. Ask: what currently limits YOUR venture?
Outcome: every successful venture must eventually scale beyond the founder.

slides.title: Capacity vs Activity
slides.body:
Activity — working harder, working alone, serving more clients yourself.

Capacity — building systems, developing people, serving more families.
slides.speakerNotes:
Walk comparison table in Playbook. Which side describes their venture today?
Introduce leadership, systems, and multiplication as growth strategies.
discussionQuestions: Which side describes your current venture?

slides.title: The Growth Engine Canvas
slides.body:
How will your venture grow beyond yourself?

Develop Leaders · Build Systems · Increase Capacity · Expand Market · Long-Term Vision
slides.speakerNotes:
Act 2 workshop (60 min). Squads open Playbook → Growth Engine Canvas.
Update FEC Box 6 as they draft.
discussionQuestions: What must you build so growth does not depend on you alone?

slides.title: The Numbers That Drive Growth
slides.body:
Year 1 Revenue Goal
÷ Average Revenue per Client
= Clients Needed

Then cascade to monthly and weekly activity.
slides.speakerNotes:
Act 3 (75 min). Walk formula on screen. Interns complete Business Growth Targets in Playbook.
Integrate 10→5→3→1→₱10,000→3 Business Engine funnel.
discussionQuestions: Can your Business Engine realistically achieve your target?

slides.title: Growth Strategy
slides.body:
How will you create more capacity?

Productivity · Technology · Systems · Partnerships · Advisors · Team
slides.speakerNotes: If numbers don't work — what must change? Stress-test assumptions in squads.
discussionQuestions: Which growth strategy fits your venture?

slides.title: FEC Growth Engine — Three Years
slides.body:
Year 1 — Launch
Year 2 — Expand
Year 3 — Multiply
slides.speakerNotes: FEC Box 6 integration. Describe how the venture grows over three years — not products, but capacity and impact.

slides.title: Squad Stress Test
slides.body:
Each squad reviews each member's calculations.

Challenge assumptions. Stress-test numbers. Suggest improvements.
slides.speakerNotes: Group activity before pitch prep. Coach circulates — probe for realism, not optimism.

slides.title: Venture Pitch Readiness
slides.body:
Friday: 5-minute Venture Pitch.

Finalize FEC · Business Engine · Growth Engine.

Investor Demo Day — a complete Financial Entrepreneurship Venture.
slides.speakerNotes:
Act 4 (30–45 min). Each participant shares revenue goal, clients, growth strategy, capacity plan.
Close: I know exactly how my advisory venture will grow.
discussionQuestions: What is the one number you are committing to?
